title: Listening - Part 3: Holiday Accommodation
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about holiday accommodation they chose. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker chose their accommodation. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about holiday accommodation they chose. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker chose their accommodation. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about choosing somewhere to stay on holiday.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: For my birthday weekend, I searched for somewhere memorable rather than a standard hotel. Prices mattered, of course, but I was prepared to pay a little more for an interesting experience. [19]When I saw that we could spend two nights in a converted lighthouse, with the old circular staircase and views from the lantern room, I booked immediately.[/19] The weather was terrible, so we didn't explore much, but staying in that building was an adventure in itself.||Speaker 2 Man: My partner and I adopted a dog last year, and we didn't want to leave her with anyone while we went away. Several nice apartments refused animals, while others charged ridiculous extra fees. [20]We finally chose a cottage where the owner genuinely welcomed dogs and even provided a secure garden where ours could run around safely.[/20] It wasn't near the beach, as we'd originally hoped, but seeing the dog enjoy herself made that unimportant.||Speaker 3 Woman: I travelled to Germany with a friend, and we wanted to take day trips to several towns rather than stay in one place all week. A beautiful country hotel caught our attention, but we would have needed a car. [21]In the end, we booked a fairly ordinary apartment because it was two minutes from the station, with frequent trains in every direction.[/21] It proved an excellent decision; we managed four different trips without any complicated planning.||Speaker 4 Man: Because of food allergies, eating in restaurants can be complicated for me; I have to ask questions about every dish, and mistakes can spoil a holiday. My family offered to find specialist hotels, but those were expensive. [22]I preferred an apartment with a proper kitchen, where I could make meals using ingredients I trusted whenever I needed to.[/22] We still ate out occasionally, but having that option removed all the anxiety and made the trip enjoyable.||Speaker 5 Woman: My friends and I decided that our summer holiday should include surfing lessons. We found cheaper rooms inland, but none of us wanted to carry boards on buses before an early morning class. [23]The small hostel we selected was directly opposite the surf school, so we could walk across the road in our wetsuits and be in the water within minutes.[/23] The rooms were basic, but after hours in the sea, all we wanted was a shower and a bed.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: For my birthday weekend, I searched for somewhere memorable rather than a standard hotel. Prices mattered, of course, but I was prepared to pay a little more for an interesting experience. When I saw that we could spend two nights in a converted lighthouse, with the old circular staircase and views from the lantern room, I booked immediately. The weather was terrible, so we didn't explore much, but staying in that building was an adventure in itself.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: My partner and I adopted a dog last year, and we didn't want to leave her with anyone while we went away. Several nice apartments refused animals, while others charged ridiculous extra fees. We finally chose a cottage where the owner genuinely welcomed dogs and even provided a secure garden where ours could run around safely. It wasn't near the beach, as we'd originally hoped, but seeing the dog enjoy herself made that unimportant.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I travelled to Germany with a friend, and we wanted to take day trips to several towns rather than stay in one place all week. A beautiful country hotel caught our attention, but we would have needed a car. In the end, we booked a fairly ordinary apartment because it was two minutes from the station, with frequent trains in every direction. It proved an excellent decision; we managed four different trips without any complicated planning.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: Because of food allergies, eating in restaurants can be complicated for me; I have to ask questions about every dish, and mistakes can spoil a holiday. My family offered to find specialist hotels, but those were expensive. I preferred an apartment with a proper kitchen, where I could make meals using ingredients I trusted whenever I needed to. We still ate out occasionally, but having that option removed all the anxiety and made the trip enjoyable.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: My friends and I decided that our summer holiday should include surfing lessons. We found cheaper rooms inland, but none of us wanted to carry boards on buses before an early morning class. The small hostel we selected was directly opposite the surf school, so we could walk across the road in our wetsuits and be in the water within minutes. The rooms were basic, but after hours in the sea, all we wanted was a shower and a bed.

options.text: it was an unusual type of building
it was in a particularly quiet area
it allowed them to prepare their own meals
it was close to a sporting activity

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker chose the accommodation because it was a converted lighthouse.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The couple selected a cottage that welcomed and accommodated their dog.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The apartment's position beside a well-connected station made it the best choice.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker needed accommodation with a kitchen because preparing safe meals was important.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The hostel was chosen because it was immediately beside the surf school.
